title: Spread and Rest Operators
description: Use `...` to spread elements or collect them into arrays.

slides.title: The Spread Operator (`...`) đźŚź
slides.content:
# Spread Operator

The spread operator (`...`) allows an iterable (like an array or object) to be expanded into individual elements.

## Spreading Arrays

```javascript
const arr1 = [1, 2, 3];
const arr2 = [4, 5, 6];
const combined = [...arr1, ...arr2]; // [1,2,3,4,5,6]
```

It's often used to create shallow copies:
```javascript
const copy = [...arr1];
```

slides.title: Spreading Objects (ES2018) đź§©
The Rest Parameter (`...`) đź›‘
slides.content:
## Rest Parameter

While spread _expands_, rest _collects_ remaining elements into an array. It's used in function parameters and destructuring.

```javascript
function sum(...numbers) {
  return numbers.reduce((acc, n) => acc + n, 0);
}
console.log(sum(1, 2, 3, 4)); // 10
```

Here, `numbers` is an array containing all arguments passed.

In destructuring:
```javascript
const [first, ...rest] = [10, 20, 30, 40];
console.log(first); // 10
console.log(rest);  // [20,30,40]
```

quizData.explanation: Spread expands `arr` into individual elements, so `newArr` becomes `[1,2,3,4]`.

slides.title: Spread vs Rest â€“ Same Symbol, Different Context đź”„
slides.content:
| Spread | Rest |
|--------|------|
| Expands an iterable into elements | Collects elements into an array |
| Used in array/object literals, function calls | Used in function parameters and destructuring |
| `...[1,2,3]` â†’ `1,2,3` | `...args` collects arguments |

Both use `...`, so context matters!
